title: Win the Heart Gift Guide
content: Special free gifts can be used as bargaining chips to close a deal when a customer is hesitating or to surprise a loyal customer and make them feel valued.

title: Win the Heart Gift Guide
content: Junior Exec has no access yet. Senior Exec has the highest quota because they are the main sales engine. Team Lead has a limited quota because their role focuses more on leadership and smooth operations.

title: Win the Heart Gift Guide
content: One customer can only receive one gift. Do not stack gifts or mix Gift A and Gift B for the same customer.

title: Win the Heart Gift Guide
content: Free gifts apply only to regular-priced and non-discounted items. Do not use them on items already on sale or under promotion.

title: Win the Heart Gift Guide
content: For purchases above RM500 on Daily, Premium, and Infused items, customer can receive Free Juice with maximum 2 bottles. Promo code: KCHJUICE. Jungle Bee quota: 20 per month. King Bee quota: 5 per month.

title: Win the Heart Gift Guide
content: For purchases above RM500 on Daily, Premium, and Infused items, customer can receive Free Mini with maximum 2 jars. Promo code: FDS. Jungle Bee quota: 20 per month. King Bee quota: 5 per month.

title: Win the Heart Gift Guide
content: After giving the gift, staff must immediately log it in the system, select 'Win Customer’s Heart' as the category, and ensure the details are accurate before submitting.

title: Win the Heart Gift Guide
content: Follow the full guide in Gift and Compensation System (Lark Tutorial) if further recording guidance is needed.

title: How shifts arrangements are given
content: This guide explains how shifts are assigned, why some staff get more shifts than others, and how SP (Shift Priority) affects scheduling.

title: How shifts arrangements are given
content: Shifts are assigned based on SP (Shift Priority), operational needs, and team balance.

title: How shifts arrangements are given
content: Each working day consists of one full shift up to 8 hours. Half Morning is 9:30 AM to 1:30 PM, Morning is 9:30 AM to 6:30 PM, Half Afternoon is 1:00 PM to 6:00 PM, Afternoon is 1:00 PM to 10:00 PM, and Evening is 6:00 PM to 10:00 PM.

title: How shifts arrangements are given
content: Staff will not be scheduled from morning until night unless approved by the Team Lead for operational reasons.

title: How shifts arrangements are given
content: The lower your SP, the more likely you are to get desirable shifts and more work opportunities.

title: How shifts arrangements are given
content: A lower SP means higher priority status and first access to desirable shifts. A higher SP means lower priority status, fewer shifts, or less flexibility.

title: How shifts arrangements are given
content: Operational Performance: staff who can handle both front-of-house and backend tasks become more versatile and more useful for different shifts, which helps lower SP.

title: How shifts arrangements are given
content: Commitment Volatility: frequent shift amendments or last-minute changes increase management cost and push SP higher.

title: How shifts arrangements are given
content: Management and Training Cost: learning quickly, following instructions, and avoiding repeated mistakes helps keep SP lower.

title: How shifts arrangements are given
content: Team Lead Selection: being selected as a Team Lead shows reliability and capability and gives maximum priority for future shifts.

title: How shifts arrangements are given
content: Reliability plus versatility leads to the best shifts. Staff should aim to be the go-to person who can handle tasks without repeated guidance.

title: How shifts arrangements are given
content: Draft Schedule: prepared based on SP, availability, and operational requirements. Staff may adjust availability before finalization.

title: How shifts arrangements are given
content: Finalized Schedule: once locked, it must be followed. Changes after finalization require Team Lead approval through the Shift Amendment Form.

title: How shifts arrangements are given
content: Staff must check the availability sheets regularly. Sheet location: Wiki homepage to Time Availability Excel Sheets.

title: How shifts arrangements are given
content: Be reliable and arrive for scheduled shifts on time.

title: How shifts arrangements are given
content: Keep your availability updated.

title: How shifts arrangements are given
content: Communicate issues early. Last-minute changes are discouraged.

title: How shifts arrangements are given
content: Understand that operations come first and flexibility is essential.

title: How shifts arrangements are given
content: Follow instructions carefully to minimize errors.

title: How shifts arrangements are given
content: Your shifts reflect your performance, accountability, and contribution.

title: How shifts arrangements are given
content: Strong performers get more opportunities, while poor performance reduces shifts and flexibility.

title: How shifts arrangements are given
content: SP is dynamic and reviewed regularly, so consistent improvement matters.

title: Bee Point Policy – Crew Member Guideline
content: The Bee Point system is for paid transactions only. No payment means no points, and payment means the transaction is eligible for points.

title: Bee Point Policy – Crew Member Guideline
content: Customers buy juice, return bottles, earn Bee Points, and redeem rewards. This creates a purchase, return, and reward loop.

title: Bee Point Policy – Crew Member Guideline
content: Crew members cannot earn Bee Points when the drink was taken as a free staff entitlement or when the bottle comes from non-paid consumption.

title: Bee Point Policy – Crew Member Guideline
content: This rule prevents double dipping, where someone gets a free drink and also claims points from it.

title: Bee Point Policy – Crew Member Guideline
content: Crew members can earn Bee Points only if they personally paid for the drink, the transaction is recorded like a normal customer sale, and the returned bottle comes from that paid purchase.

title: Bee Point Policy – Crew Member Guideline
content: When those strict conditions are met, the crew member is treated as a customer, not staff.

title: Bee Point Policy – Crew Member Guideline
content: Crew members are allowed a free drink based on company policy, for example 1 per day.

title: Bee Point Policy – Crew Member Guideline
content: Bottles from free drinks cannot be used for Bee Points.

title: Hari Raya Aidilfitri Public Holiday – Retail (2026)
content: Based on the government announcement dated 15 March 2026, the Hari Raya public holiday schedule varies depending on the official date of Hari Raya.

title: Hari Raya Aidilfitri Public Holiday – Retail (2026)
content: If Hari Raya falls on Friday 20 March 2026, then 20 March 2026 is Hari Raya Day 1, 21 March 2026 is Hari Raya Day 2, 23 March 2026 is Additional Public Holiday plus Johor Sultan’s Birthday for Johor only, and 24 March 2026 is the replacement holiday for Johor only.

title: Hari Raya Aidilfitri Public Holiday – Retail (2026)
content: If Hari Raya falls on Saturday 21 March 2026, then 20 March 2026 is Additional Public Holiday, 21 March 2026 is Hari Raya Day 1, 23 March 2026 is Johor Sultan’s Birthday for Johor only, and 24 March 2026 is the replacement holiday for Hari Raya Day 2 for Johor only.

title: Hari Raya Aidilfitri Public Holiday – Retail (2026)
content: The 23 and 24 March holidays apply to Johor only. Retail staff must follow the final roster arrangement by management. The Additional Public Holiday depends on the confirmed actual Hari Raya date.

title: Hari Raya Aidilfitri – Dress Code
content: Staff are allowed to wear baju raya during Hari Raya Aidilfitri.

title: Hari Raya Aidilfitri – Dress Code
content: Attire must remain neat, clean, professional, and suitable for customer-facing roles.

title: Hari Raya Aidilfitri – Dress Code
content: Avoid overly flashy or inappropriate outfits. Avoid clothing that restricts movement during work.

title: Hari Raya Aidilfitri – Dress Code
content: Ensure the outfit is comfortable for shift duties and does not affect service performance.

title: Hari Raya Aidilfitri – Dress Code
content: All staff must follow the dress guidelines.

title: Hari Raya Aidilfitri – Dress Code
content: Team Leader must ensure team members comply with professional standards.

title: Hari Raya Aidilfitri – Dress Code
content: Celebration should not affect service quality. Maintain Jungle House brand image at all times.

title: Kuching incentive data submission
content: To keep the incentive system fair, transparent, and smooth for everyone, crew members must follow the submission guidelines carefully. Precision and honesty are essential.

title: Kuching incentive data submission
content: Crew members must refer to the final sales report PDF when filling in their incentive declaration. The 10PM sales update is only a snapshot and extra transactions may still happen after that time.

title: Kuching incentive data submission
content: Sample final sales report reference image for incentive declaration.

title: Kuching incentive data submission
content: All incentive declarations must be completed within 24 hours. The clock starts at 10:00 PM on the day of the shift.

title: Kuching incentive data submission
content: Late submissions are not allowed. After the 24-hour window, the system locks and the crew member can no longer fill in the data.

title: Kuching incentive data submission
content: Submission is not required if the crew member did not have a shift that day or has no incentive points to declare.

title: Kuching incentive data submission
content: All incentive data must be keyed in with extra care and full attention.

title: Kuching incentive data submission
content: No re-keying is allowed. If the wrong date or wrong details are entered, the incentive is forfeited for that day because the system does not allow corrections.

title: Kuching incentive data submission
content: Crew members should regularly double check their entries. If mistakes are discovered, Eno must be informed immediately.

title: Kuching incentive data submission
content: There is zero tolerance for cheating. Any attempt to manipulate the system results in losing the entire month’s incentive, and serious cases may lead to a formal warning or a police report.

title: Kuching incentive data submission
content: Reliability matters. Repeated or careless errors may lead to losing your weekly incentive and raise concerns about your professionalism.

title: Kuching incentive data submission
content: Accuracy is a reflection of your integrity. Attention to detail is how trust is built with each other.

title: Kuching incentive data submission
content: Let’s make sure every point earned is truly deserved.

title: Kuching incentive data submission
content: It’s awesome bee-ing with you!
